incident_reconstruction.impact_direction: Frontal impact, likely from a stationary or slow-moving vehicle ahead
incident_reconstruction.reconstruction: The Tata Altroz XZ Plus was likely traveling in moving traffic when the vehicle ahead stopped suddenly. The driver was unable to react in time, resulting in a frontal collision with the vehicle ahead. The impact caused moderate front-side damage.
reconstruction_bullets: The damage pattern indicates a frontal impact, which is inconsistent with the claim of hitting the rear of another vehicle.
The lack of rear damage on the vehicle ahead or any witness statements supporting the claim raises suspicions about the incident's authenticity.
The overall story consistency verdict is low due to the mismatch between the claimed scenario and the physical damage evidence.

storyboard_panels.title: Pre-Incident
storyboard_panels.description: The Tata Altroz XZ Plus was traveling in moving traffic, likely in a urban setting.

storyboard_panels.title: Point of Impact
storyboard_panels.description: The vehicle ahead stopped suddenly, and the Tata Altroz XZ Plus collided with it, causing frontal damage.

storyboard_panels.title: Immediate Aftermath
storyboard_panels.description: The driver assessed the damage, which appeared to be moderate front-side damage.

storyboard_panels.title: Evidence Summary
storyboard_panels.description: The physical evidence suggests a frontal impact, which does not match the claimed scenario of hitting the rear of another vehicle.

inconsistencies: Frontal damage inconsistent with claim of rear-end collision
No evidence of rear damage on the vehicle ahead
incident_reconstruction.summary: Most Probable: Front impact | Confidence: 40% | Story Match: No

reasoning_trail: Step 1: Damage Assessment revealed no visible damage, contradicting the claim description
Step 2: Fraud Intelligence indicated a low fraud score but raised concerns with physics mismatch and possible staged collision scenario
Step 3: Incident Reconstruction showed damage does not match the story, further raising suspicions
Step 4: Context & Policy verification could not confirm the incident location, adding to the uncertainty
Step 5: Historical Precedents, such as HIST-014, highlight the importance of verifying damage consistency and location
Step 6: Settlement Calculation is not applicable due to the rejection decision
Step 7: Final Decision to reject the claim based on the cumulative evidence of inconsistencies and unverifiable information
